metadata.summary: Python package for interacting with Sumo in an FMU setting

documentation:
# fmu-sumo

[![Documentation Status](https://readthedocs.org/projects/fmu-sumo/badge/?version=latest)](https://fmu-sumo.readthedocs.io/en/latest/?badge=latest)


## Documentation and guidelines
:link: [fmu-sumo documentation](https://fmu-sumo.readthedocs.io/en/latest/)


## Contribute
[Contribution guidelines](./CONTRIBUTING.md)

modules.name: fmu
documentation: This line ONLY! :

modules.name: fmu.sumo.explorer.Filters
documentation: Filter that matches 4d-seismic objects.

modules.name: fmu.sumo.explorer.explorer
documentation: Module containing class for exploring results from sumo

classes.name: fmu.sumo.explorer.explorer.Explorer

methods.name: fmu.sumo.explorer.explorer.Explorer.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the Explorer class

Args:
    env (str): Sumo environment
    token (str): authenticate with existing token
    interactive (bool): authenticate using interactive flow (browser)
    keep_alive (str): point in time lifespan (deprecated and ignored)

methods.name: fmu.sumo.explorer.explorer.Explorer.get_permissions
documentation:
Get permissions

Args:
    asset (str): asset in Sumo

Returns:
  dict: Dictionary of user permissions

methods.name: fmu.sumo.explorer.explorer.Explorer.get_permissions_async
documentation:
Get permissions

Args:
    asset (str): asset in Sumo

Returns:
  dict: Dictionary of user permissions

Class for consuming FMU results from Sumo.
The Sumo Explorer is a Python package for consuming FMU results stored
in Sumo. It is FMU aware, and creates an abstraction on top of the
Sumo API. The purpose of the package is to create an FMU-oriented
Python interface towards FMU data in Sumo, and make it easy for FMU
users in various contexts to use data stored in Sumo.

Examples of use cases:
  - Applications (example: Webviz)
  - Scripts (example: Local post-processing functions)
  - Manual data browsing and visualization (example: A Jupyter Notebook)

modules.name: fmu.sumo.explorer.timefilter
documentation: Module with classes handling time filtering

classes.name: fmu.sumo.explorer.timefilter.TimeType
documentation:
An Enum representing diffent time types in Sumo

Used when creating a TimeFilter object.

classes.name: fmu.sumo.explorer.timefilter.TimeFilter

methods.name: fmu.sumo.explorer.timefilter.TimeFilter.__init__
documentation:
Initialize TimeFilter

Args:

: time_type (TimeType): time type (TIMESTAMP, INTERVAL, ALL, NONE)
  start (str): start of range
  end (str): end of range
  overlap (bool): include overlapping intervals
  exact (bool): include only exact matches

Examples:

> Get surfaces with timestamps:
>
> ```
> time = TimeFilter(time_type=TimeType.TIMESTAMP)
>
> case.surfaces.filter(time=time)
> ```
>
> Get surfaces whith timestamp in range:
>
> ```
> time = TimeFilter(
>     time_type=TimeType.TIMESTAMP,
>     start="2018-01-01",
>     end="2022-01-01"
> )
>
> case.surfaces.filter(time=time)
> ```
>
> Get surfaces with intervals:
>
> ```
> time = TimeFilter(time_type=TimeType.INTERVAL)
>
> case.surfaces.filter(time=time)
> ```
>
> Get surfaces with intervals in range:
>
> ```
> time = TimeFilter(
>     time_type=TimeType.INTERVAL,
>     start="2018-01-01",
>     end="2022-01-01"
> )
>
> case.surfaces.filter(time=time)
> ```
>
> Get surfaces where intervals overlap:
>
> ```
> time = TimeFIlter(
>     time_type=TimeType.INTERVAL,
>     start="2018-01-01",
>     end="2022-01-01",
>     overlap=True
> )
>
> case.surfaces.filter(time=time)
> ```
>
> Get surfaces with exact interval match:
>
> ```
> time = TimeFilter(
>     time_type=TimeType.INTERVAL,
>     start="2018-01-01",
>     end="2022-01-01",
>     exact=True
> )
>
> case.surfaces.filter(time=time)
> ```

Class representing a time filter

A TimeFilter object can be used when doing time filtering on case objects.

methods.name: fmu.sumo.explorer.objects._metrics.Metrics.min
documentation:
Find the minimum value for the specified property across the
current set of objects.

Arguments:
    - field (str): the name of a property in the metadata.

Returns:
    The minimum value.

methods.name: fmu.sumo.explorer.objects._metrics.Metrics.max
documentation:
Find the maximum value for the specified property across the
current set of objects.

Arguments:
    - field (str): the name of a property in the metadata.

Returns:
    The maximum value.

methods.name: fmu.sumo.explorer.objects._metrics.Metrics.avg
documentation:
Find the average value for the specified property across the
current set of objects.

Arguments:
    - field (str): the name of a property in the metadata.

Returns:
    The average value.

methods.name: fmu.sumo.explorer.objects._metrics.Metrics.sum
documentation:
Find the sumo of all values for the specified property across
the current set of objects.

Arguments:
    - field (str): the name of a property in the metadata.

Returns:
    The sum of all values.

methods.name: fmu.sumo.explorer.objects._metrics.Metrics.value_count
documentation:
Find the count of values for the specified property across the
current set of objects.

Arguments:
    - field (str): the name of a property in the metadata.

Returns:
    The total number of values.

methods.name: fmu.sumo.explorer.objects._metrics.Metrics.cardinality
documentation:
Find the count of distinct values for the specified property
across the current set of objects.

Arguments:
    - field (str): the name of a property in the metadata.

Returns:
    The number of distinct values.

Note: The value returned is approximate.

methods.name: fmu.sumo.explorer.objects._metrics.Metrics.stats
documentation:
Compute a basic set of statistics of the values for the specified
property across the current set of objects.

Arguments:
    - field (str): the name of a property in the metadata.

Returns:
    A dictionary of statistical metrics.

methods.name: fmu.sumo.explorer.objects._metrics.Metrics.extended_stats
documentation:
Compute an extended set of statistics of the values for the
specified property across the current set of objects.

Arguments:
    - field (str): the name of a property in the metadata.

Returns:
    A dictionary of statistical metrics.

methods.name: fmu.sumo.explorer.objects._metrics.Metrics.percentiles
documentation:
Find the values at specific percentiles for the specified
property across the current set of objects.

Arguments:
    - field (str): the name of a property in the metadata.
    - percents ([number]): list of percent values. If omitted, uses
      a default set of values.

Returns:
    A dictionary of percentiles.

functions.name: fmu.sumo.explorer.objects._search_context._gen_filter_id
documentation: Match against document id(s) (in uuid format).

documentation:
Match property against either single value or list of values.
If the value given is a boolean, tests for existence or not of the property.

functions.name: fmu.sumo.explorer.objects._search_context._gen_filter_name
documentation: Match against "data.name", or "case.name" for case objects.

functions.name: fmu.sumo.explorer.objects._search_context._gen_filter_time
documentation: Match against a TimeFilter instance.

functions.name: fmu.sumo.explorer.objects._search_context._gen_filter_bool
documentation: Match boolean value.

functions.name: fmu.sumo.explorer.objects._search_context._gen_filter_complex
documentation:
Match against user-supplied query, which is a structured
Elasticsearch query in dictionary form.

methods.name: fmu.sumo.explorer.objects._search_context.SearchContext.select
documentation:
Specify what should be returned from elasticsearch.
Has the side effect of clearing the lru cache.
sel is either a single string value, a list of string value,
or a dictionary with keys "includes" and/or "excludes" and
the values are lists of strings. The string values are nested
property names.

Args:
    sel (str | List(str) | Dict(str, List[str]): select specification
Returns:
    None
